summary: DHH expresses excitement about the upcoming Red Hot Chili Peppers album, their 9th, due for release on July 9th, 2002. He reflects on the three years since Californication, noting he was running a gaming website during the dot-com bubble at the time. He praises the first single 'By the Way' for its vocals, lyrics, and guitar work. He sets modest expectations, saying even half the great tracks of Californication would keep his fandom intact, and laments missing their Roskilde Festival performance.
key_points: Three years have passed since Californication, which DHH considers his entry point into RHCP fandom
The first single from the upcoming album impresses with vocals, lyrics, and guitar riffs
DHH sets a reasonable bar: half the killer tracks of Californication would satisfy him
A brief aside reveals his dot-com bubble era running a Quake 3 gaming website
money_quotes: Anthony Kiedis' voice is still engrossing, the lyrics still sends my thoughts running, and the riff still brings me to the edge of what I can take while enjoying it.
Should it contain just half the amount (3) of killer tracks as Californication (6), RHCP will easily retain my fandom and I humbly sit tight for another three years.
Back then, I was a year into my Bubble Dream persuit, running a gaming website by the name of Quake3.dk.
key_insight: A rare purely personal post from 2002 showing DHH as a music fan eagerly awaiting the next Red Hot Chili Peppers album, with a passing glimpse into his pre-Rails dot-com era life.
